page: Microsoft Azure E112ibds_v5 is an isolated virtual machine instance belonging to the EIBDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 112 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 56 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, with cache sizes of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3. The instance is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, and includes 3891 GB of local storage.

Benchmark data shows strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores all placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, with read-only small block, read-only large block, and write large block tests consistently ranking in the top 25%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU encryption tests reach top-tier status within the top 10%. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression scores falling in the middle 50%.

While the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, it exhibits notable performance degradation in large-scale machine learning tasks. LLM inference speed for text generation using a large 70B model falls into the bottom 10%, indicating a clear tradeoff for workloads requiring heavy GPU-less deep learning inference. Given the high memory density, substantial local storage, and strong multi-threaded performance, this instance is suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, relational databases, and high-throughput data processing workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure E112ibds_v5 is an isolated virtual machine instance from the EIBDSv5 family, powered by a 112-vCPU Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 672.0 GB of RAM and 3891 GB of local storage. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier multi-threaded decompression and encryption capabilities, all placing in the top 25% or top 10%. Conversely, single-core performance and single-threaded operations remain average. A significant tradeoff is observed in large-scale LLM inference, where text generation speeds for a 70B model fall into the bottom 10%. This instance is suited for memory-intensive enterprise databases, large-scale software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E112ibds_v5 is an isolated instance with 112 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 3891 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E112ibds_v5 features 112 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 3891 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: An isolated x86_64 platform featuring high memory density, substantial local storage, and strong multi-core processing for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 112 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 672 GB of RAM
3891 GB of local storage with high block storage performance
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Top-tier multi-threaded decompression and encryption performance
Poor performance for large-scale LLM text generation tasks
Suited for memory-intensive databases and enterprise applications
